---
title: "doppel"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "12/30/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(vegan)
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(agricolae)
library(FSA)
library(rcompanion)
library(data.table)
library(doppelgangerIdentifier)
```


```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Load the data
# Adjust the file paths if they are not in your current working directory
df_species <- read_excel("data/doppel_data/doppel_species.xlsx")
df_clinical <- read_excel("data/doppel_data/meta_diabetes.xlsx")

# 2. Prepare the IDs for matching
# For species, we assume the ID is in the first column. 
# We explicitly rename the first column to 'match_id' temporarily for easier handling.
colnames(df_species)[1] <- "match_id"

# For clinical, the ID is 'FREG1_Barcode'.
# We convert both to character strings to ensure they match correctly (e.g., "500" vs 500).
df_species$match_id <- as.character(df_species$match_id)
df_clinical$FREG1_Barcode <- as.character(df_clinical$FREG1_Barcode)

# 3. Find common IDs
common_ids <- intersect(df_species$match_id, df_clinical$FREG1_Barcode)

message(paste("Found", length(common_ids), "common rows."))

# 4. Filter both dataframes
df_species_filtered <- df_species %>%
  filter(match_id %in% common_ids) %>%
  arrange(match_id) # Optional: Sort by ID

df_clinical_filtered <- df_clinical %>%
  filter(FREG1_Barcode %in% common_ids) %>%
  arrange(FREG1_Barcode) # Optional: Sort by ID

# 5. Save to new files
write.csv(df_species_filtered, "data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(df_clinical_filtered, "data/doppel_data/meta_diabetes_filtered.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

diabetes
```{r}
# 1. Read the filtered CSV files
df_species_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df_clinical_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/meta_diabetes_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 2. Set Row Names
# For the species file, use the 'match_id' column
rownames(df_species_clean) <- df_species_clean$match_id
df_species_clean$match_id <- NULL  # Remove the column since it's now the index

# For the clinical file, use the 'FREG1_Barcode' column
# (Note: These values should match the species row names now)
rownames(df_clinical_clean) <- df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode
df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode <- NULL # Remove the column since it's now the index

# 3. Verify
# Check that the row names are identical and in the same order
identical(rownames(df_species_clean), rownames(df_clinical_clean))

```
```{r}
df_species_clean
df_species_clean_t = transpose(df_species_clean)
rownames(df_species_clean_t) = colnames(df_species_clean)
colnames(df_species_clean_t) = rownames(df_species_clean)
df_species_clean_t
```


```{r}
diabetes1 = getPPCCDoppelgangers(df_species_clean_t, df_clinical_clean)

visualisePPCCDoppelgangers(diabetes1)
```
```{r}
subset(diabetes1$PPCC_df, DoppelgangerLabel == "Doppelganger")
```

obesity
```{r}
# 1. Read the filtered CSV files
df_species_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df_clinical_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/meta_obesity.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 2. Set Row Names
# For the species file, use the 'match_id' column
rownames(df_species_clean) <- df_species_clean$match_id
df_species_clean$match_id <- NULL  # Remove the column since it's now the index

# For the clinical file, use the 'FREG1_Barcode' column
# (Note: These values should match the species row names now)
rownames(df_clinical_clean) <- df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode
df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode <- NULL # Remove the column since it's now the index

# 3. Verify
# Check that the row names are identical and in the same order
identical(rownames(df_species_clean), rownames(df_clinical_clean))
df_clinical_clean
```
```{r}
df_species_clean
df_species_clean_t = transpose(df_species_clean)
rownames(df_species_clean_t) = colnames(df_species_clean)
colnames(df_species_clean_t) = rownames(df_species_clean)
df_species_clean_t
```


```{r}
obesity1 = getPPCCDoppelgangers(df_species_clean_t, df_clinical_clean)

visualisePPCCDoppelgangers(obesity1)
```
```{r}
subset(obesity1$PPCC_df, DoppelgangerLabel == "Doppelganger")
```
## chinese vs non
```{r}
# 1. Read the filtered CSV files
df_species_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df_clinical_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/meta_chinese.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 2. Set Row Names
# For the species file, use the 'match_id' column
rownames(df_species_clean) <- df_species_clean$match_id
df_species_clean$match_id <- NULL  # Remove the column since it's now the index

# For the clinical file, use the 'FREG1_Barcode' column
# (Note: These values should match the species row names now)
rownames(df_clinical_clean) <- df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode
df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode <- NULL # Remove the column since it's now the index

# 3. Verify
# Check that the row names are identical and in the same order
identical(rownames(df_species_clean), rownames(df_clinical_clean))
df_clinical_clean
```
```{r}
df_species_clean
df_species_clean_t = transpose(df_species_clean)
rownames(df_species_clean_t) = colnames(df_species_clean)
colnames(df_species_clean_t) = rownames(df_species_clean)
df_species_clean_t
```


```{r}
chinese1 = getPPCCDoppelgangers(df_species_clean_t, df_clinical_clean)

visualisePPCCDoppelgangers(chinese1)
```
```{r}
subset(chinese1$PPCC_df, DoppelgangerLabel == "Doppelganger")
```

## indian vs non
```{r}
# 1. Read the filtered CSV files
df_species_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df_clinical_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/meta_indian.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 2. Set Row Names
# For the species file, use the 'match_id' column
rownames(df_species_clean) <- df_species_clean$match_id
df_species_clean$match_id <- NULL  # Remove the column since it's now the index

# For the clinical file, use the 'FREG1_Barcode' column
# (Note: These values should match the species row names now)
rownames(df_clinical_clean) <- df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode
df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode <- NULL # Remove the column since it's now the index

# 3. Verify
# Check that the row names are identical and in the same order
identical(rownames(df_species_clean), rownames(df_clinical_clean))
df_clinical_clean
```
```{r}
df_species_clean
df_species_clean_t = transpose(df_species_clean)
rownames(df_species_clean_t) = colnames(df_species_clean)
colnames(df_species_clean_t) = rownames(df_species_clean)
df_species_clean_t
```


```{r}
indian1 = getPPCCDoppelgangers(df_species_clean_t, df_clinical_clean)

visualisePPCCDoppelgangers(indian1)
```
```{r}
subset(indian1$PPCC_df, DoppelgangerLabel == "Doppelganger")
```

## female vs male
```{r}
# 1. Read the filtered CSV files
df_species_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/doppel_species_filtered.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df_clinical_clean <- read.csv("data/doppel_data/meta_female.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 2. Set Row Names
# For the species file, use the 'match_id' column
rownames(df_species_clean) <- df_species_clean$match_id
df_species_clean$match_id <- NULL  # Remove the column since it's now the index

# For the clinical file, use the 'FREG1_Barcode' column
# (Note: These values should match the species row names now)
rownames(df_clinical_clean) <- df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode
df_clinical_clean$FREG1_Barcode <- NULL # Remove the column since it's now the index

# 3. Verify
# Check that the row names are identical and in the same order
identical(rownames(df_species_clean), rownames(df_clinical_clean))
df_clinical_clean
```
```{r}
df_species_clean
df_species_clean_t = transpose(df_species_clean)
rownames(df_species_clean_t) = colnames(df_species_clean)
colnames(df_species_clean_t) = rownames(df_species_clean)
df_species_clean_t
```


```{r}
female1 = getPPCCDoppelgangers(df_species_clean_t, df_clinical_clean)

visualisePPCCDoppelgangers(female1)
```
```{r}
subset(female1$PPCC_df, DoppelgangerLabel == "Doppelganger")
```
## food and doppel

1 helios (ethnic and microbiome)
2 food and microbiome (ffq and infogest + ASA24)
3 helios diabetes obesity and risk score
4 helios doppel + crc ibd public data doppel + review paper
